title: Smoky Roasted Quinoa Stir-Fry
description: A smoky and indulgent low-calorie-friendly American dish featuring fluffy quinoa with mixed grains in a light Asian-inspired sauce.

ingredients.name: mixed grains (brown rice, millet, or quinoa)
fresh herbs (cilantro or parsley, finely chopped) (optional)

instructions: Rinse quinoa and mixed grains thoroughly in a fine-mesh strainer.
In a medium saucepan, bring 2 cups of water to a boil. Add quinoa and mixed grains, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 15 minutes or until the liquid is absorbed.
Meanwhile, heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic cloves and sauté for 30 seconds or until fragrant. Stir in grated ginger and cumin seeds; cook for an additional minute.
Stir in smoky paprika and chili flakes (if using). Cook for another minute to toast the spices.
Add soy sauce, rice vinegar, and cooked quinoa mixture into the skillet with vegetables. Stir well to combine all ingredients evenly.
Reduce heat to low and cook for 5-7 minutes or until sauce thickens slightly and grains are fully coated.
Transfer the dish onto a plate. Sprinkle with sesame seeds and chopped fresh herbs if desired before serving.
